activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Founded India's first accessible EdTech platform for visually impaired youth. Partnerships with Youth for Jobs & National Association of the Blind. 2,000+ children taught coding & English.
activities.Skills Applied: Social entrepreneurship, curriculum design, public policy, inclusion advocacy
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: 2,000+ visually impaired kids | 2 national org partnerships | India's first blind-youth-led public policy forum | Global Winner – Global Student Cognitive Challenge

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Head of Editorial for India's first nationwide blind-youth-led journalism & public policy summit, organised under Learnable.
activities.Skills Applied: Editorial leadership, event management, journalism, inclusion advocacy
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: First-of-its-kind national event bridging journalism + public policy for visually impaired youth.

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Journalist for 5 years, then led the full editorial team as Editor-in-Chief in the final year.
activities.Skills Applied: Investigative journalism, editorial leadership, writing, team management
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: Best Journalist Award × 4 | Led entire editorial team | 6-year commitment to school's media identity

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Published 3 books – 2 available worldwide, 1 won the Emily Dickinson Award. TEDx talk at ISH. Deloitte speaker.
activities.Skills Applied: Creative writing, literary research, public speaking, storytelling
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: Emily Dickinson Award winner | 2 books available globally | TEDx at ISH | Deloitte speaker

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Led content strategy for a youth mental health NGO for 3 years – campaigns, articles, educational material.
activities.Skills Applied: Content strategy, mental health communication, copywriting, campaign management
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: 3 years leading youth-focused mental health awareness content for a national NGO

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Led content in Year 1, then led the entire organisation as President in Year 2.
activities.Skills Applied: Content leadership, organisational management, strategic planning
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: Scaled from content lead to President; drove organisational strategy and growth

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Led content creation, strategy, and editorial direction for 4 consecutive years.
activities.Skills Applied: Long-term content leadership, editorial strategy, team collaboration

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Prefect for 4 years, then Deputy Head Prefect – ran poetry/debate competitions; hosted CHIREC's first literary & cultural festival (400+ participants).
activities.Skills Applied: Leadership, event management, peer mentorship, public speaking
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: Hosted CHIREC's first literary & cultural fest (400+ participants) | 4 years of institutional leadership

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Independently conducted and published academic research through SRNN.
activities.Skills Applied: Research methodology, academic writing, critical analysis
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: Published peer-recognised research paper on SRNN | Demonstrates independent scholarly capability

activities.Description (150 chars for Common App): Delivered a TEDx talk on the power of literature and the writing journey.
activities.Impact & Key Outcomes: Selected as TEDx speaker – reached local and online audience

honors.Description: Won the Emily Dickinson Award for published book.
Awarded Best Journalist four consecutive times.

honors.Honor / Award Name: Global Winner – Global Student Cognitive Challenge
honors.Description: Won global prize for work with Learnable.

honors.Why It Matters: Highest tier recognition at international convention.

honors.Description: Selected as Ashoka Young Scholar – global social-impact fellowship.
Published independent academic research on SRNN.

audit.Verified correct value: 2026-27 tuition is $72,500 (full cost of attendance ~$98,000). Student figure ~$7k low.

audit.ChatGPT / tracker value: Nov 1 REA + Jan 2 RD; can't apply ED/EA elsewhere
audit.Verified correct value: Single-Choice (Restrictive) Early Action Nov 1 (non-binding) + RD Jan 2. Restriction confirmed.
Test-required for Fall 2027 entry (SAT or ACT). Yale ended test-flexible May 2026.
Confirmed need-blind for international applicants; meets 100% need without loans.
Yale publishes score bands not a single composite; Class of 2029 ~ EBRW 760-800 / Math 760-800. ~1500-1580 is a fair estimate.
Correct it is a BA; Yale Economics is not formally STEM-CIP designated (no STEM OPT advantage).
Wharton BS-Econ is NOT automatically STEM. Only specific concentrations are STEM (Finance, Statistics, Business Analytics, BEPP, OIDD, etc.). Must pick a STEM concentration for 24-mo OPT.
2026-27 tuition $65,670 (+ fees; total charges ~$94,600). ~$1.3k high.

audit.ChatGPT / tracker value: Nov 1 ED I binding + Jan 5 RD
audit.Verified correct value: ED Nov 1 (binding) + RD Jan 5. Penn has one ED round (not ED I/II).
Penn requires SAT or ACT (testing reinstated).
Need-aware for international applicants; must apply for aid at admission (can't add later).
Class of 2029 ~ EBRW 740-770 / Math 770-800 -> ~1510-1570. Low end ~20 pts low.
ED is ~Nov 3 and RD is Jan 5 (not Nov 1 / Jan 2). ED binding is correct.
2026-27 tuition $73,740 (total charges ~$96,600). ~$10k low.
Duke is TEST-OPTIONAL for 2026-27 (applies to international students too).
Duke BS Economics is STEM (CIP 45.0603) -> 24-mo STEM OPT. (Even the AB is STEM-coded.)
Need-aware for a limited number of internationals; meets 100% need once admitted.
Official Class of 2029 reported range 1520-1570.
ED Nov 1 (binding) + RD Jan 2, per Dyson.
Apply via Common App selecting 'Cornell SC Johnson College of Business - Dyson.' Dyson is shared between SC Johnson & CALS - 'CALS not A&S' framing is imprecise.
International students pay the ENDOWED rate $73,946 (2026-27). The ~$66k likely confused the NY-resident contract rate, which internationals can't use.
Cornell requires SAT/ACT for Fall 2026+ (testing reinstated).
Need-aware for internationals; meets 100% need for admitted internationals.
Dyson AEM is widely STEM-designated, but the exact CIP could not be confirmed on an official Cornell page - confirm with Cornell directly.
Could not confirm from an official Cornell source (Cornell publishes limited score data).
Emory Economics is a STEM-CERTIFIED BA, not a BS. STEM claim holds; 'BS' is wrong. (Joint Econ-Human Health is NOT STEM.)
2026-27 tuition $70,300 (total charges ~$93,100). ~$10k low.
ED I Nov 1, RD Jan 1 (also ED II Jan 1). Applies to Emory College and Oxford.
Emory is TEST-OPTIONAL through the 2026-27 cycle.
Need-based aid for a select group of internationals. IMPORTANT: internationals must apply REGULAR DECISION to be considered for need-based aid (not via ED).
~100 pts low. Class of 2029 ~ EBRW 740-770 / Math 760-800 -> ~1500-1570 (Oxford slightly lower).
Can apply to Oxford College, Emory College, or both; same Emory degree, Oxford students move to Atlanta by junior year.
UC is TEST-BLIND - does not consider SAT/ACT at all in admissions or scholarships. 'SAT Optional' and the per-campus SAT ranges should be removed (they are not used in UC admission).
Filing window OPENS Oct 1; submission deadline Nov 30. (Not Nov 1-30.) One UC app covers all campuses - correct.
2026-27 nonresident tuition ~ $54,858; with campus fees ~$56,700; total budget ~$86,000/yr. ~$44k is outdated (2023-24).
Internationals generally ineligible for UC need-based aid and loans; pay full nonresident cost.
UCLA Economics & Business Economics reclassified STEM from Fall 2019 (CIP 45.0603).
Berkeley Economics reclassified STEM (CIP 45.0603) ~2019.
Multiple UCSD econ majors are STEM (BA Econ 'EN25', BS Econ, Management Science) - all STEM-OPT eligible, not only Business Econ.
Econ & Accounting is confirmed STEM; the plain Economics BA major sheet doesn't state STEM - confirm with UCSB OISS for the specific major.
UC Davis Economics reclassified STEM (CIP 45.0603) in 2019.
Pattern supports Quantitative Economics being the STEM major (vs plain Econ BA), but official econ pages don't state it explicitly - confirm with UCI before relying on it.
LSA undergrad Economics is a BA and is NOT STEM-designated (only the grad Master of Applied Economics is STEM). No 24-mo STEM OPT - major flag for a STEM-focused student.
EA Nov 1 non-binding + RD Feb 1 confirmed. Note Michigan now ALSO offers a binding Early Decision (also Nov 1) - distinguish the two.
Michigan is TEST-OPTIONAL for 2026-27.
2025-26 nonresident tuition & fees ~ $63,962 (lower div) / $68,444 (upper div); total budget ~$84-89k. 2026-27 higher. ~$58k too low.
Consistent with reported enrolled middle-50% (~1360-1530); descriptive only since test-optional.
ASEAN Undergraduate Scholarship is for ASEAN nationals only. India is NOT in ASEAN - student is INELIGIBLE at all three. Remove 'ASEAN' everywhere.
MOE Tuition Grant requires a 3-year bond with a Singapore-registered employer after graduation.
SGD 9,350 is the Singapore CITIZEN rate. For Indian (All-Other-International) the subsidised fee is ~ SGD 21,400/yr.
Non-subsidised fee ~ SGD 36,650/yr (Humanities & Sciences band).
International cycle opens ~early Dec and CLOSES ~23 Feb. March is too late.
'NUS Merit' is Singapore-citizen-only. International equivalents: NUS Global Merit / Science & Technology Undergraduate Scholarship.
IB Math courses are AA and AI (there is no 'AB'). NUS wants HL Math / Math AA.
BSc (Hons) Data Science & Economics under College of Humanities & Sciences.
SGD 8,950 is the Citizen rate. Indian (All-Other-International) subsidised fee ~ SGD 20,000/yr; non-subsidised ~ SGD 36,250.
NTU's standalone Economics degree is a Bachelor of Social Sciences (Hons) in Economics - not a BSc. (A separate BSc Economics & Data Science exists.)
International application closes ~15 March; opens earlier (~Oct).
SGD ~11,550 is the Citizen rate. Indian (Other-International) subsidised fee ~ SGD 26,200/yr; non-subsidised ~ SGD 47,700.
Bachelor of Science (Economics); shortlisted applicants attend interview/test (Mar-May). Apply by ~19 Mar, scores by ~31 Mar.
Global Impact Scholarship Award is open to international students. (ASEAN one is not - see above.)
2026 BCom international fee ~ AUD 55,000-58,000/yr (BCom Hons ~AUD 57,856). ~48k too low.

audit.ChatGPT / tracker value: Melbourne Intl UG Scholarship - up to 100% tuition + stipend
audit.Verified correct value: It is a fee remission only (no stipend): 25% pre-2027, dropping to 20% fee sponsorship from 2027. Not 100%+stipend.
BCom has Feb and July intakes (July/Sem-2 valid). IELTS 6.5 (no band <6.0). Exact 31 May deadline unconfirmed.
Bachelor of Economics 2027 indicative Year-1 fee = AUD 59,100. Too low.
That award is POSTGRADUATE-only. The UG equivalent is the 'Sydney Scholars India Scholarship Program.'
Vice-Chancellor's International Scholarships Scheme exists (merit-based, commencing UG/PG).
Bachelor of Economics has both Semester 1 (Feb/Mar) and Semester 2 (Aug) intakes - pick one; tracker is internally inconsistent.
UNSW BCom international indicative fee ~AUD 50,000+/yr (2026/27). Confirm on degree page.
All Indian students eligible for the 20% International Student Award (every year). Scientia = full tuition or AUD 20,000/yr.
UNSW uses trimesters (Term 1 ~Feb, 2 ~May/Jun, 3 ~Sep); early offers Dec/Jan consistent.
Monash BCom (B2001) international fee ~ AUD 52,200/yr (2026). Too low.

audit.ChatGPT / tracker value: Intl Merit Scholarship - up to AUD 10,000/yr
audit.Verified correct value: Monash International Merit Scholarship is AUD 15,000 per annum (for the duration), not 10,000.
Aug-2025 intake annual tuition = ₹10,22,700; with residence/services total ~₹12.5L/yr. ₹8L is wrong; ~₹10.7L tuition is right.

audit.ChatGPT / tracker value: R1 Nov 2026 / R2 Jan / R3 Mar 2027
audit.Verified correct value: Ashoka runs FOUR rounds. 2027-intake dates not yet published - months are a reasonable estimate; mark as 'to be confirmed.'

audit.ChatGPT / tracker value: Ashoka Scholar Award - up to 100% tuition
audit.Verified correct value: Ashoka offers up to 100% need-based tuition waivers + merit scholarships (500 announced for 2026 intake). Confirm the exact award name.
Application includes essays/SOP; shortlisted candidates attend a personal interview.
2026 Year-I program (tuition) fee = ₹8,75,000 (rising to ₹10L by Year IV). Total Year-I with lodging ~ ₹11,45,000. ₹5L is ~half actual.
Scheduled rounds (not pure rolling). Selection: FEAT aptitude test + essay + personal interview (SAT/ACT can waive FEAT).
Merit awards (partial to full waivers) + need-based assistance. 'Up to 50%' actually understates it (full waivers exist).

bigErrors: TUITION IS UNDERSTATED ON ALMOST EVERY SCHOOL. The cost sheet uses old/low figures. Current 2026-27 tuition is materially higher: Yale $72.5k, Penn $65.7k, Duke $73.7k, Cornell $73.9k, Emory $70.3k, UC nonresident ~$54.9k (total ~$86k), Michigan ~$64k+.
TEST POLICY ERRORS: Duke, Emory and Michigan are TEST-OPTIONAL (not 'SAT required'). The UC system is TEST-BLIND - it ignores SAT entirely, so the SAT ranges listed for UCs are not used in admission.
STEM MISLABELS: Michigan undergrad Economics is a BA and NOT STEM (no STEM OPT). Wharton BS-Econ is only STEM if you pick a STEM concentration. Emory Econ is a STEM-certified BA, not a 'BS'.

SINGAPORE FEES WRONG: the 'with-grant' figures are Singapore-CITIZEN rates. As an Indian student Mehek pays the international subsidised rate - roughly 2-3x: NUS ~S$21,400, NTU ~S$20,000, SMU ~S$26,200/yr.
ASEAN SCHOLARSHIP DOESN'T APPLY: India is not an ASEAN country, so the 'ASEAN scholarship' listed for NUS/NTU/SMU is not available to her. Remove it.

SCHOLARSHIP ERRORS (Australia): Melbourne's scholarship is a 20% fee remission (not '100% + stipend'); Sydney's 'India Equity' award is postgraduate-only (UG = 'Sydney Scholars India Scholarship Program'); Monash merit award is AUD 15,000/yr (not 10,000).
DUKE DEADLINES are ~Nov 3 ED / Jan 5 RD (not Nov 1 / Jan 2).
INDIA FEES low: Ashoka tuition ~₹10.2L (total ~₹12.5L); FLAME tuition ₹8.75L (total ~₹11.45L), not ₹5L.
